text: "Honestly, the thing I care most about is making sure the writing feels real — like, grounded in actual stories from actual people, not just statistics floating in a vacuum. I want donors to feel something, but I never want it to tip into guilt-tripping or emotional manipulation — there's a real line there and I'm pretty careful about it. The tone should be warm and human but still professional, you know? And every piece needs to end with a clear, specific ask — I can't stand when appeals just kind of... trail off without telling people what to do next."
